statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.description: The total number of minutes played.

statistics.name: assistTurnoverRatio
statistics.description: The average number of assists a player or team records per turnover

statistics.description: The total number of rebounds (offensive and defensive).

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

statistics.description: The number of times when the offense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot.

article.description: James Daniel III scored 35 points and James Miller score 21 to help Howard cruise to a 106-64 win over Division III Medgar Evers on Friday night.
article.headline: Daniel III, Miller lead Howard past Medgar Evers, 106-64
article.linkText: Daniel III, Miller lead Howard past Medgar Evers, 106-64
article.story: WASHINGTON -- James Daniel III scored 35 points and James Miller score 21 to help Howard cruise to a 106-64 win over Division III Medgar Evers on Friday night. Daniel made 9 of 14 field goals, including 4 of 7 from 3-point range, and 13 of 13 free throws, while Miller made 8 of 13 from the field. Solomon Mangham added 13 points and seven rebounds for Howard (1-2). It was the Bison's highest scoring output since a 116-111 win over VMI on Nov. 25, 2006. A pair of free throws by Medgar Evers' Matthew Lee tied it at 23 with 8:58 left in the first half but Howard rattled off 15 of the next 19 points and closed the half on a 16-3 run to lead 57-36 at the break. The Cougars trailed by at least 20, and as many as 46, the rest of the way. Maurice Swaby II led Medgar Evers with 17 points.
